content.problemStatement:
Write a program to declare and accept an list of NNN(**will always be greater than 3**) space separate integers. 

Calculate the **multiplication** of the 1st and 3rd elements of the list and output the same to the console.

Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a list of N space‑separated integers, where 4 ≤ N ≤ 1000. The integers can be negative, zero or positive and fit in a signed 32‑bit range.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the product of the first and third elements of the given list.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First element is 10, third element is 25; 10 × 25 = 250.
First element is -3, third element is -2; -3 × -2 = 6.
First element is 0, third element is 5; 0 × 5 = 0.

solution.approach: For each test case read the whole line, split it by whitespace and convert the tokens to 64‑bit integers. The first token is the first element, the third token is the third element. Multiply them using a 64‑bit type (long long / long / Python int / BigInt) and output the result. The algorithm is O(N) per test case for reading, but only the first three numbers are actually used for the computation.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of integers) – reading dominates, multiplication is O(1).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space per test case (ignoring input buffer).
